---
type: fact
situation: When building a command-line application in Go
subject: Go CLI cross-cutting quality conventions
predicate: combine atomic crash-safe storage, aligned dual-mode output, color auto-detection, and a canonical feature set
object: 'Storage: XDG data dir (/<app> → ~/.local/share/<app>), atomic writes (os.CreateTemp in same dir + os.Rename), separate JSON files for live/undo/archive items, Load() silently returns empty slice on first run. Output: list --json emits raw JSON for machines; default is a column-aligned table with padding computed from plain-text widths (never ANSI widths); ANSI color for priority/status/overdue auto-disabled when NO_COLOR env set or stdout is not a TTY (os.ModeCharDevice). Feature set: add (--tag, --priority, --due), list (--tag filter, --json, --archived), search (case-insensitive substring across all text fields + tags), edit/rename, tag (deduplicated), start/archive (status transitions), stats (counts + rate + overdue), undo (snapshot-before-mutate). Tests: table-driven with MemStore + FakeClock + bytes.Buffer; t.Parallel() on every test; assert exact timestamps via injected clock; errors.Is for sentinel errors.'
luhmann: "2"
created: "2026-05-30"
source: distilled L3 generic lesson
---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Go CLI cross-cutting quality conventions combine atomic crash-safe storage, aligned dual-mode output, color auto-detection, and a canonical feature set Storage: XDG data dir (/<app> → ~/.local/share/<app>), atomic writes (os.CreateTemp in same dir + os.Rename), separate JSON files for live/undo/archive items, Load() silently returns empty slice on first run. Output: list --json emits raw JSON for machines; default is a column-aligned table with padding computed from plain-text widths (never ANSI widths); ANSI color for priority/status/overdue auto-disabled when NO_COLOR env set or stdout is not a TTY (os.ModeCharDevice). Feature set: add (--tag, --priority, --due), list (--tag filter, --json, --archived), search (case-insensitive substring across all text fields + tags), edit/rename, tag (deduplicated), start/archive (status transitions), stats (counts + rate + overdue), undo (snapshot-before-mutate). Tests: table-driven with MemStore + FakeClock + bytes.Buffer; t.Parallel() on every test; assert exact timestamps via injected clock; errors.Is for sentinel errors..
